title: Airella elegans
intro: Airella elegans, also known as Sea Oats, is a perennial grass species commonly found in coastal areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and lower cholesterol levels. The bioactive constituents of Airella elegans include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Airella elegans is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional preparations such as tonics and salves.
taxonomy: Airella elegans is a species of grass within the genus Airella. The genus Airella belongs to the family Poaceae. This family is commonly known as the grass family or Poa family. Poaceae is one of the largest plant families and includes many economically important species. It is widely distributed across the globe, with different species found in various ecosystems.
